questions.question: Explain why tangent ratio is commonly used in height and distance problems.
questions.answer: Tangent ratio is commonly used because it directly relates the height of the object and the horizontal distance from the observer.

questions.question: Why are angle of elevation and angle of depression often equal?
questions.answer: They are equal because they form alternate interior angles between two parallel horizontal lines.

questions.question: Why is observer height added in some trigonometric problems?
questions.answer: Observer height is added because the angle of elevation is measured from the observer’s eye level, not from the ground.

questions.question: Which trigonometric ratio is used when hypotenuse is known and why?
questions.answer: Sine or cosine ratio is used because they involve the hypotenuse of the triangle.

questions.question: What is the relationship between sine and cosecant?
questions.answer: Sine and cosecant are reciprocal of each other.

questions.question: Why is a ladder problem represented as a right-angled triangle?
questions.answer: Because the wall and the ground are perpendicular to each other, forming a right angle.

questions.question: What happens to the angle of elevation when the observer moves closer to the object?
questions.answer: The angle of elevation increases when the observer moves closer to the object.

questions.question: Why is trigonometry considered an indirect measurement method?
questions.answer: Because it is used to find heights and distances that cannot be measured directly.

questions.question: Which trigonometric ratio is reciprocal of cosine?
questions.answer: Secant is the reciprocal of cosine.

questions.question: Find the value of cot 30°.
Why is angle of elevation always acute?
questions.answer: Because the object is always above the horizontal level of the observer.

questions.question: What is the value of tan 90° and why?
questions.answer: tan 90° is undefined because the adjacent side becomes zero.

questions.question: Explain the importance of horizontal line in trigonometric problems.
questions.answer: The horizontal line is used as a reference line to measure angles of elevation and depression.

questions.question: Which trigonometric ratio is used when base and hypotenuse are known?
questions.answer: Cosine ratio is used when base and hypotenuse are known.

questions.question: Why is sin 90° equal to 1?
questions.answer: Because the opposite side is equal to the hypotenuse.

questions.question: What is the value of cos 90°?
Which ratio gives the steepness of a slope?
questions.answer: Tangent ratio gives the steepness of a slope.

questions.question: Why is trigonometry useful in construction work?
questions.answer: It helps in measuring heights of buildings and towers without direct measurement.

questions.question: What is the value of sin 0°?
Which ratio is reciprocal of tangent?
questions.answer: Cotangent is the reciprocal of tangent.

questions.question: Why are right triangles used in trigonometry?
questions.answer: Because trigonometric ratios are defined only for right-angled triangles.

questions.question: What is the value of tan 0°?
Which trigonometric ratio involves all three sides?
questions.answer: None of the trigonometric ratios involve all three sides together.

questions.question: Why are obtuse angles not used in basic trigonometry?
questions.answer: Because trigonometric ratios are defined for acute angles in right triangles.

questions.question: State two applications of trigonometry.
questions.answer: Trigonometry is used in surveying and construction.
